entries.title: rig
entries.description: rig — a quality-gated AI workbench for Claude Code. Give /rig:go (the main entry) a natural-language task: it classifies the task, runs it in an isolated git worktree, and checks the result against an acceptance gate — the diff only lands in your working tree on an explicit accept. Track parallel tasks with /rig:go board; compose recipes, personas, and knowledge as reusable bricks. scripts/mcp_server.py exposes the same task/orchestrate operations over a stdlib-only MCP stdio server, so an external agent, CI, or another process can drive rig without a live Claude Code session — opt-in, no new engine, accept/discard safety unchanged. scripts/workbench.py record-commit/record-outcome/trace-commit close the loop from accept-time gate predictions to real production outcomes — link a commit to its task, record ok/incident, and reverse-look-up a commit to its original gate verdict with a revert-plan draft (never auto-applied). codex/skills/rig/SKILL.md, codex/hooks.json, and .codex/agents/security-reviewer.toml wire rig into Codex CLI's own native layer (Skills/Hooks/Subagent TOML) alongside the existing skill-symlink and --provider codex paths. scripts/host_adapters.py generalizes the Codex native-layer pattern to any host (Cursor added as a second, validating example) via a single per-host capability/hook-event/degrade table — no new host needs core changes. orchestrate.py run --provider anthropic hits the Anthropic Messages API directly to detect and transparently handle Fable 5's refusal-classifier/server-side-fallback (#297), instead of a gate silently failing on it. workbench.py confidence surfaces drill-measured per-reviewer detection rate as a supplementary signal alongside the pass/fail gate, recording reviewer_confidence into acceptance.json without touching gate logic itself. workbench.py diff inserts an AST-based semantic-diff summary for modified *.py files (signature vs. body-only vs. no-semantic-change), augmenting the text diff rather than replacing it. orchestrate.py mcp-scan statically analyzes the MCP server's own tool definitions across three adversarial lenses (attacker/defender/auditor) for shell/network over-permission, secret exposure, and hook-injection risk, and is wired into validate.py for CI. action.yml packages headless CI usage of orchestrate.py run --isolate as a composite GitHub Action, opening a PR only when the gate resolves DONE. orchestrate.py ab runs the same goal through multiple recipe variants concurrently, each in its own isolated worktree, and compares speed/retries/results. workbench.py new surfaces similar past tasks (Jaccard overlap on a rough tokenization, no embeddings) above the routing banner as a deja-vu hint, and talk-mode captures decisions/confirmed-assumptions/open-questions into a per-task talk-log.md once a task-id exists. The README documents a dogfooding workflow (digest/stats/drill --replay) for maintainers to measure rig's own gate efficacy, honestly scoped as manual today (no auto-published CI badge yet). orchestrate.py fleet --repos p1,p2,... aggregates multiple repositories' run/drill telemetry read-only, comparing per-persona reviewer detection rate across projects (with an --anonymize option for consulting/multi-client use). scripts/notify.py posts opt-in Slack/Teams webhook notifications (accept-pending, gate REJECT, escalation) via urllib only, no SDK dependency. A security/quality batch adds pre-generation secret masking before subagent dispatch, a Semgrep-to-single-criterion SAST adapter, a --rescan mode for already-imported skills, known-flaky-vs-regression test discernment, and observability findings bridged into concrete instrumentation implement steps. .rig/access.json (opt-in) restricts accept to an allowlist per task_type by identity (RIG_USER or git config user.name); --budget-minutes on workbench.py new flags an over-estimate task in status/board, advisory only. Gap prescriptions (orchestrate runs) now draft a ready-to-paste /rig:forge request naming the top rejecting reviewers for a recurring escalation (#268). workbench.py accept now writes a locally-signed (HMAC-SHA256) provenance.json for each accepted task; verify-provenance checks the signature and exits 1 on tamper — same-machine tamper-evidence, not third-party public verification (#299). An experimental, opt-in orchestrate.py backend (cfg["parallel_backend"]="managed-agents") delegates review-gate fan-out to the Anthropic Managed Agents API (coordinator/worker beta) instead of the default subprocess+ThreadPoolExecutor path, verified against a mock HTTP server only (#295). vscode-extension/ is a read-only VS Code sidebar view of .rig/runs/ task/gate state (no accept/discard or any write command registered), parsing the same JSON workbench.py already writes; its state-parsing logic is unit-tested with plain Node (#286). workbench.py instincts manages .rig/instincts.jsonl, a continuous cross-session layer of lightweight, confidence-scored, unverified patterns (separate from facets/knowledge); secrets/local-paths/env-assignments are rejected outright, unused instincts decay and expire, conflicting ones are muted via explicit --supersedes, and only high-confidence ones are injected at session start via hooks/inject-instincts.sh, with hooks/suggest-instincts.sh prompting the model to propose new ones (#306). workbench.py cockpit is a read-only Mission Control screen aggregating the run timeline, gate radar, drill-measured reviewer confidence, a cost meter, and a force-bypass safety strip by reusing board/stats/audit/confidence's existing aggregation functions, with no new persistence and no accept/discard performed from cockpit itself (#307). scripts/prose_rhythm.py deterministically measures Japanese prose-rhythm surface proxies (sentence-beat runs, ending repetition, paragraph uniformity, progress narration, connective density) as an advisory pre-pass for the de-ai-smell flow, grounded in the cognitive-rhythm principle added to knowledge/ai-writing-smells.

entries.title: sapui5-cli
entries.description: Manages SAPUI5/OpenUI5 projects using the UI5 Tooling CLI (@ui5/cli). Use when initializing UI5 projects, configuring ui5.yaml or ui5-workspace.yaml files, building UI5 applications or libraries, running development servers with HTTP/2 support, creating custom build tasks or server middleware, managing workspace/monorepo setups, troubleshooting UI5 CLI errors, migrating between UI5 CLI versions, or optimizing build performance. Supports both OpenUI5 and SAPUI5 frameworks with complete configuration and extensibility guidance.

entries.title: medical-mcps
entries.description: Comprehensive biological and medical research data integration server providing unified access to 13+ specialized databases including Reactome (pathways), KEGG (pathways/diseases), UniProt (proteins), ChEMBL (drug discovery), PubMed (literature), GWAS Catalog (genetic associations), ClinicalTrials.gov (clinical trials), OpenFDA (drug safety), Pathway Commons (pathway data), Gene Ontology (gene functions), OMIM (genetic disorders), NCI Thesaurus (cancer terminology), OpenTargets (disease-target associations), and CTG (gene-disease). Offers 100+ tools through both a unified endpoint (all databases via single connection) and individual API endpoints. Features RFC 9111 compliant HTTP caching with 30-day TTL for optimal performance.

entries.title: nature-skills
entries.description: A growing collection of Claude-compatible academic workflow bundles for producing work at Nature-journal standard. Covers scientific figures (nature-figure), manuscript prose polishing (nature-polishing), manuscript drafting and methods writing (nature-writing), reviewer-style pre-submission assessment (nature-reviewer), citation retrieval and export (nature-citation), data availability statements and FAIR metadata (nature-data), paper-to-PPTX presentation conversion (nature-paper2ppt), literature search via MCP (nature-academic-search), paper reading and annotation (nature-reader), and peer-review response drafting (nature-response). Future releases planned: statistical reporting, cover letters, and review articles. Rules are derived from primary sources, including published Nature papers, journal author guidelines, and structured writing curricula.

entries.description: Dungeon Master assistant for running persistent D&D 5e campaigns. entries.description: 小红书笔记自动创作技能：撰写内容、生成精美封面与正文卡片、可选一键发布。支持 8 种排版主题（sketch / neo-brutalism / botanical 等）和 4 种智能分页模式。

entries.title: bigtable
entries.description: Official Claude plugin for Google Cloud Bigtable. Manage instances/tables, design schemas, query data using SQL or client libraries, and more

entries.title: ai-sdlc-harness
entries.description: AI-driven SDLC workflow harness for multi-repo development. Provides story refinement, multi-agent development (plan → code → review → test → PR), and automated guardrails via hooks. Supports Azure DevOps, Jira, GitLab, GitHub, Zoho, and local Markdown as work item providers; ADO, GitLab, GitHub, gh-cli, and glab-cli as git providers. Language-agnostic, discovery-driven; supports any language (frontend + backend).

entries.title: appdirect
entries.description: Agent skills for AppDirect marketplace work — app submission runbook (100% profile-completion) and platform API integration (OAuth, GraphQL mutations, event endpoints, SSO).

entries.title: fleet
entries.description: Claude Code supervises a multi-provider fleet of AI coding workers (OpenAI Codex, Google Gemini CLI, Claude Code headless, and 75+ providers via OpenCode including DeepSeek, Qwen, and local LM Studio/Ollama models) as background subagents: registry-driven routing by capability, cost, and observed track record; parallel dispatch; per-provider rate-limit cooldowns with cascade fallback; verification-gated acceptance; and an outcome ledger that makes routing smarter with use. NOTE: runs unattended agents (file edits + command execution, no approval prompts) inside your repo — read the README warning before installing; use only in repos you trust. The skill itself follows the Agent Skills open standard and also runs under Codex CLI, Gemini CLI, Cursor, and other compatible supervisors (see install.sh).
